entry.title: Re: Unique employee number based user creation in Identity directory service

entry.title: Re: Issue is in DME, While migrating classic to Sepa dme...
entry.content:
Hello,
You can create a custom fm for BTE 2040 according to your requirement with your own logic (see also SAP note 1033893 and 1224437) if report RFFOAVIS_FPAYM is used

entry.title: Assignment of Scoped roles in Datasphere via cloud identity services
entry.content:
Hi,
Is it possible to assign custom Scoped roles in SAP Datasphere via Cloud identity services- Identity authentication as source and SAP Datasphere as Target system?

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 2 — Roll & Move
entry.content:
Your srv/game-service.js handler implementations (paste as a JS code block)

const cds = require('@SAP/cds')

module.exports = class GameService extends cds.ApplicationService {
  async init() {

    const { Players, BoardSquares } = this.entities

    // ── startTurn ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Waiting]  →  CAP validates status before this runs
    // @TO: #Playing      →  CAP updates status after this returns
    // No business logic needed — CAP's @flow.status handles the transition.
    this.on('startTurn', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Intentionally empty: the @from/@to annotations do all the work.
    })

    // ── rollDice ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Playing]  →  CAP validates status before this runs
    // @TO: #Moving       →  CAP updates status after this returns
    this.on('rollDice', 'Players', async (req) => {
      const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

      const roll = Math.ceil(Math.random() * 6)
      let newPosition = player.position + roll

      // Can't overshoot square 100 — must land exactly
      if (newPosition > 100) {
        await UPDATE(Players).set({ lastRoll: roll }).where({ ID: player.ID })
        const needed = 100 - player.position
        return req.reject(409, `Need exactly ${needed} to finish. Rolled ${roll}. No move.`)
      }

      // Resolve snake or ladder on the new square
      const square = await SELECT.one.from(BoardSquares).where({ square: newPosition })
      let finalPosition = newPosition
      let event = null

      if (square?.ladderTo) {
        finalPosition = square.ladderTo
        event = { type: 'ladder', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
      } else if (square?.snakeTo) {
        finalPosition = square.snakeTo
        event = square.isDoubleHead
          ? { type: 'doubleSnake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
          : { type: 'snake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
      }

      // Persist position and last roll
      await UPDATE(Players)
        .set({ position: finalPosition, lastRoll: roll })
        .where({ ID: player.ID })

      // Emit domain event so subscribers (Week 3) can react
      if (event) await this.emit('BoardEvent', { playerID: player.ID, ...event })

      // Use req.reply() — not return — to pin the response value before CAP's
      // @flow.status after-handler runs. A plain `return` gets cleared by the
      // flow machinery, producing HTTP 204 instead of the expected 200 + body.
      req.reply({ roll, position: finalPosition, event: event?.type ?? 'normal' })
    })

    // ── confirmMove ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  normal end of a turn, hands off to next player (Week 3)
    // @TO: #Waiting     →  player waits for their next turn
    this.on('confirmMove', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Week 3 will add turn rotation here.
      // For now: just let CAP apply @TO: #Waiting
    })

    // ── blockPlayer ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  called when player lands on a double-headed snake
    // @TO: #Blocked     →  player skips their next turn
    this.on('blockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Business logic: the position was already updated in rollDice.
      // This action just transitions state — CAP handles the @TO: #Blocked update.
      req.info('Player is blocked and will skip their next turn.')
    })

    // ── unblockPlayer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Blocked]   →  called at the start of a blocked player's next turn
    // @TO: $flow.previous →  CAP restores the status to whatever it was before #Blocked
    this.on('unblockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // CAP's $flow.previous does the work — no code needed here.
      // The player's status returns to #Waiting automatically.
    })

    // ── winGame ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  called when player.position === 100
    // @TO: #Finished    →  player has won
    this.on('winGame', 'Players', async (req) => {
      const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

      // Mark the session as finished with this player as winner
      await UPDATE('snakeladder.GameSessions')
        .set({
          finishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
          winner_ID: player.ID
        })
        .where({ ID: player.session_ID })

      await this.emit('GameWon', { playerID: player.ID, sessionID: player.session_ID })
    })

    return super.init()
  }
}
 
waiting status after confirmMove

entry.title: Badi not found for check validation for batch with respect to storage location
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We have the following business requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud:

During Production Order Confirmation for an SFG material, the user enters both a storage location and a batch number for the produced material.

Business rule:

  • Storage Location SOR1 should only allow batches starting with SOR (e.g., SOR1001).
  • Storage Location SIL1 should only allow batches starting with SIL (e.g., SIL1001).

Example:

  • Storage Location = SOR1

  • Batch = SOR1001 → Allowed

  • Storage Location = SOR1

  • Batch = SIL1001 → System should issue an error message and prevent saving the confirmation.

We are looking for a suitable BAdI  that can be triggered during Production Order Confirmation to validate the combination of storage location and batch number before the confirmation is saved.

Could you please advise which released BAdI can be used for this requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Regards, 
Name-Sagar Nagose

entry.title: Re: BTP ABAP Environment: Inbound Service - System to System
entry.content: Seems from their tutorial, basic authentication using the communication user is the recommended way? Or am I missing something? https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-a4c-inbound-communication..html#3c0aea2b-edf0-47c9-b7ef-fbd5e67b3712

entry.title: Re: F4 help for Application Job in Public Cloud (2502 release)
entry.content: Is this feature available for Private cloud 2025 version ?

entry.title: Re: PAYM_ADV OPD Config is not Working for Custom Service
entry.content:
Hi,
Using unreleased SAP standard classes inside your custom development directly violates the Clean Core paradigm.
Best regards,
Mónika

entry.title: Re: Migrate to SCIM API for better User Identity Sync between SuccessFactors and IAS/IPS
entry.content:
@PrinceThomas Hello -- did SAP ever answer your questions about existing API integrations?  It seems like these discussion boards raise a lot of good questions that are left unanswered.  Also, if you don't mind me asking, are you using a contract partner to do the SCIM migration?  The documentation I've been reading seems daunting for someone with only 3 years experience supporting SAP software.  

Thank you - BK

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC EDI / IDOC Support
entry.content:
Hi Maurice,

you may want to listed to the DRC Germany webinars here under events: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/document-reporting-compliance

EN 16931 is the required format, how it is exchanged in many ways is left up to sender/receiver. 

DRC does not support EDI transactions today as SAP's current focus is on current and nextGen technology.

entry.title: Re: RFC adapter in Integration Suite receives data from SAP backend but drops payload
entry.content:
For anyone that might go through this issue, upon further investigation, I found out that there were special characters coming from the backend SAP system (e.g., incorrectly encoded characters in unicode, which resulted in a 'square' being in the string of a specific field of the record being exposed in the RFC exporting table parameter). Therefore, from this record onwards, upon deserialization when receiving the XML payload back from the RFC, it throws a silent exception, dropping the content.

The fix is to guarantee that there are no special, incorrectly encoded characters in any text field in the data in the backend. this solved the issue for me.

 

thanks,

entry.title: Re: Migrate to SCIM API for better User Identity Sync between SuccessFactors and IAS/IPS
entry.content:
@PrinceThomas , @bkelley62 

This is only a change to the API version being used in Cloud Identity Services. Our Learning users authenticating via BizX were not impacted and saw no change.

entry.title: Career Roadmap: Seeking the Definitive Learning Order for SAP S/4HANA MM + EWM Consultant/Specialist
entry.content:
I am currently planning my career path to become an SAP S/4HANA MM (Materials Management) and EWM (Extended Warehouse Management) Consultant (Specialist) . While there is plenty of information on the individual modules, I am struggling to find a definitive "Learning Order" that connects both worlds effectively.

I want to move beyond just "knowing the transactions" to understanding the embedded integration and process design required for a Specialist.

My Ultimate Goal:
To be proficient in end-to-end processes (Procure-to-Pay and Warehouse Operations) within the S/4HANA ecosystem, including handling complex physical logistics.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to our SAP Learning Group moderated by SAP Learning Experts
entry.content:
Hi  @Margit_Wagner 

I’m a beginner in the SAP ecosystem. I finished an SAP Bootcamp late last year and fell in love with this universe.

I come from a sales/commercial background and am currently studying Accounting. My focus now is finding the best path to keep learning and growing in SAP.

I’m so happy to join you all, and I can't wait to learn, grow, and connect with this entire community!

entry.title: Re: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 5)
entry.content:
Hi Martin, 

so here is my question:

How do you think to use MCP Servers with SAP, and what is your recommendation how to configure the access? 

Especially read/write access.

Additional, what about the agents. I read a lot about having some agents that observe the activity of each other. Is there a good starting point on how to start and what agents should be in place?

Of course, all questions out of the perspective as a developer 🙂

~Florian

entry.title: Copying the files to my source tree
entry.content:
Hi, ALL,

 

In the SQL Anywhere nstaller there is a folder called SDK/dbcapi.

 

This folder contains 3 helper files and the samples folder.

 

Am I permitted to copy those 3 files into my own source tree? Or I have to reference then from its original place?

 

Thank you..

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | Restriction Field: User Group
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I create a role for the administrator of company 5140 to manage users belonging to company 5140.
Setup:

  • Business Role ID: ZBRS_5140_ADMINISTRATOR
  • The values ​​of Access Categories are Restricted.
  • The values of User Group field value are ZCB5140_USER. User Group is Leading Restriction.
  • Other field values are blank.

I think the User Group field supports displaying or changing user data.

The administrator with the role ZBRS_5140_ADMINISTRATOR can do the following (based on what I've tried):

  • View all user data on the system (even if those users are not in User Group ZCB5140_USER)

 

  • Edit user data belonging to User Group ZCB5140_USER
  • Cannot edit user data belonging to other User Groups

Question:

  • Why can this administrator view all user data on the system (even if those users are not in User Group ZCB5140_USER)
  • Is my role setup correct?

Thank you very much.

QuocDT5

entry.title: Cannot find datasource 0COUNTRY_ATTR in SAP ERP DEV or BWD
entry.content:
Cannot find 0COUNTRY_ATTR datasource in SAP ERP DEV.  Not in ROOSOURCE.

Need to replicate it to BWD

SAP Support recommended that I used this datasource to load the BW T005 tables.

entry.title: Re: Sap ewm: Error during mapping: material does not exist
entry.content:
If the error "Error during mapping: material XYZ does not exist"
Then check the entries in the table "/SCWM/TMAPSTLOC"
If the mapping is not done correctly for the logical system and the Sloc, Follow the below path
SPRO – Extended Warehouse Management - Interfaces - ERP Integration – Goods Movements - Map Storage Locations from ERP System to EWM

entry.title: Re: Processing Electronic Invoices in France with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content: @AdilHoussa  as you can see from your screenshot, the e-doc is created belong the wrong process 'France EU eInvoice'. If you want to submit to PPF and Peppol through PA process, the e-doc needs to be created under 'Domestic customer invoice'. You must correct/add missing configurations and create new e-documents. I'm afraid you have the incorrect setup and/or incorrect master data (for B2B invoices, you need to maintain valid SIREN/SIRET and remove the customer from EDOEUBUPAV). Please review the documentation and you might need some consulting support if you are struggling with the setup itself. This is not a product issue, but rather incorrect setup in your system.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
Re: RAP managed early internal numbering and static factory action - retrieve automatic guid
entry.content:
Dear JaimeRodriguez and rammel_sapdev,

thanks for your replies. This works just as intended. 

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: Processing Electronic Invoices in France with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
@MaeW  you actually need to initiate registration and sign an agreements. Details can be found here: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/creating-participant-for-your-company-f291a5bcafee4889a80e06fb974ef4e3?locale=en-US&version=Cloud

This was also presented in last week Webinar. Replay: https://event.on24.com/wcc/r/5398959/572E6C7DE0C5253DDCF8463CCC7350CB

For queries, I would recommend to exchange in the country-specific workzone that is maintained for this purpose: https://workzone.one.int.sap/site#workzone-home&/groups/CK8AhwChqYjMisy8oYSfgv/workpage_tabs/LxnjcOht6DIhuitgncZLhH

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community! My name is Aditya Sai Rohith Kaki, and this is my journey into SAP

entry.title: export data from sap bods using api method
entry.content:
Hello,

using sap bods 4.3, can we export data to external using using api method. source is from sap bw and the external vendor prefers api method. can you share any documentation or steps needed to perform this data export.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types
Error AADSTS90072 when assign practical system S4C01 Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to share my experience issue when tried an access an SAP Learning Practice System and would like to know if is any one experiencing the same issue?

When I access the practice system URL: https://my414802.s4hana.cloud.sap/ui

I am using my corporate Microsoft account:

Sign in

Sorry, but we’re having trouble signing you in.

 
AADSTS90072: User account 'xxxxx@xxx.com' from identity provider 'https://xxxxxxxx' does not exist in tenant 'SAP SE' and cannot access the application 'https://accounts.sap.com/msec'(SAP SE: SAPID UnifiedAuthentication PROD MSEC) in that tenant. 

The version private seems working "SAP Access Control Implementation and Configuration"

I opened a case and since last week no response from sap learning support!

Had anyone face same problem?

I appreciate any guidance or shared experience.
 
Best regards,
Wellington

entry.title: VA01 vertical scrollbar visible only after horizontal scrolling
entry.content:
Hi,

I am using an SAP test system based on Global Bike as part of my studies. I noticed a usability issue in transaction VA01.

When the SAP GUI window is maximized, the right-side vertical scrollbar is visible and the bottom horizontal scrollbar is not needed. However, when the window is reduced, the bottom horizontal scrollbar appears and the vertical scrollbars move to the far right side.

I have also seen a case in another S/4HANA system where these scrollbars were displayed even when the SAP GUI window was maximized. In that case, the user had to move the bottom horizontal scrollbar to the right in order to access the vertical scrollbar. After doing this, the left part of the screen was cut off and fields/tabs such as Order Reason were no longer visible.

This is not convenient when creating sales orders where the Order Reason field needs to be used, because the user has to scroll horizontally just to access the vertical scrollbar, and then loses visibility of the left side of the screen.

I checked a similar VA01 layout in SE51. The main screen was SAPMV45A / 4001,  and the lower area was defined as SUBSCREEN_BODY.

My questions are:

  1. From a technical point of view, can this behaviour be changed by adjusting the dynpro/subscreen layout in SE51/SE80?
  2. Is the scrollbar position controlled by the width/position of the lower subscreen area, for example SUBSCREEN_BODY?
  3. Or is the scrollbar position fully controlled by SAP GUI / standard SAP screen behaviour?
  4. Why does the screen not adjust to the available SAP GUI window size in the same way as in the old SAP system?

I understand that changing SAPMV45A  would be a standard modification, but I would like to understand what technically controls the position of this vertical scrollbar and why the screen does not resize in the same way as in the legacy SAP system.

If this is not the correct board, please advise where this SAP GUI / dynpro layout question should be posted.

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: export data from sap bods using api method
entry.content: I don’t think SAP BODS directly exposes an API endpoint for exporting data. Usually, BODS is used to extract data from BW, transform it, and then deliver it through methods like files, database tables, or web services. If the vendor requires an API, you may need to create an API layer and have BODS feed that layer. Check the vendor’s required API format first, then choose the best integration approach.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 03 - Declarative field control with @mandatory
Re: SAP Joule for Materials Management (MM) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content: Great Xenia!! Joule help to perform any task easily!

entry.title: Re: Demystifying Model Context Protocol (MCP), Part 2: From Concept to Code
entry.content:
Hello Vaidya,

With Joule Orchestrator & Agent Gateway, i assume it is also possible to discover correct Odata service & its  entity/action/Function & perform a Inbound ODATA API call. Is there a usecase to have MCP on top of ODATA API? 

Br, Rakesh

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | Restriction Field:...
entry.content:
Thanks for the question QuocDT5.
Short summary
What you observe is expected behavior in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
The User Group restriction mainly controls write (and partly read for specific operations), but it does not always restrict the initial list/display of users in the Maintain Business Users app.

✅ Why the administrator can still view all users

This is due to how the authorization concept works:
  • SAP uses a “granting authorizations” model (not denying), where restrictions define what you are allowed to do, not explicitly what must be hidden.
    → This means absence of restrictions does not automatically block visibility. [help.sap.com]
  • Restrictions are evaluated per IAM app and per access category (Read / Write / Value Help).
    → The system determines behavior based on the combination of IAM apps and restriction types. [help.sap.com]
  • In your specific case:
    • The role contains multiple IAM apps:
      • Display Business Users (F1303...) → read
      • Maintain Business Users (F1303...) → write
    • The User Group restriction is applied, but:
      • It is enforced when performing operations (e.g., edit/change)
      • It is not strictly enforced for list display in all scenarios
👉 Result:
  • The user can see all users (list-level visibility)
  • But can only edit users within ZCB5140_USER (as your test confirms)

✅ Is your role setup correct?

✔️ Yes — your setup is technically correct based on standard behavior:
  • Access Categories = Restricted ✔️
  • User Group as Leading Restriction ✔️
  • Write/Edit limited to ZCB5140_USER ✔️
👉 Your test results actually confirm the restriction is working correctly:
  • ✅ Edit restricted → works as designed
  • ⚠️ Display unrestricted → standard system behavior (not an error)

⚠️ Important design limitation

  • In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, many restriction fields (including User Group) are primarily designed for controlling data processing, not always UI-level filtering of result lists.
  • The system may allow broad visibility but controlled action permissions, especially in administrative apps.

✅ Recommended options (if stricter visibility is required)

Since hiding users completely is your goal, consider:
  1. Check assigned IAM apps carefully
    • Remove or limit Display Business Users if not needed
    • Use only apps aligned with your restriction concept
  2. Review additional restriction types
    • Some catalogs include different restriction objects (e.g., Business User (CLASS))
    • Behavior depends on the exact catalog + IAM app combination
  3. Validate combined role assignments
    • If the user has multiple roles, unrestricted access in one role can override restrictive ones
      → This is explicitly documented behavior [help.sap.com]
  4. Consider organizational/process solution
    • Full list-level filtering for users by group is limited by design
    • You may need to complement with procedures or custom apps (if applicable)

✅ Sources

  • How to Define Authorizations Based on Restrictions (SAP Help)
  • Maintain Business Roles (SAP Help)
  • IAM Apps and Authorization Variants (SAP Help)

🤖 AI transparency note

This response was prepared with AI‑powered assistance and validated against official SAP documentation current at the time of response. Please always verify against your customer's system release.
If the response satisfies your request, please mark it accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 04 - Namespaces, contexts and name prefixes
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
Re: Badi not found for check validation for batch with re...
entry.content:
Dear @munishgoel,

We could validate one released extensibility option for production order confirmation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: BAdI “Change of Goods Movement Data” in business context Manufacturing: Order Confirmation (PP_ORDER_CONFIRMATION). SAP Help documents this BAdI for influencing goods movement data during confirmation, specifically to add/delete components for goods issues or change quantities for automatic goods receipts.
That said, from the SAP Help sources we checked, we could not validate a released BAdI that is explicitly documented for enforcing a storage location ↔ batch prefix validation and raising an error before saving the confirmation.
So the practical recommendation is:
  • first, review the released BAdI documentation in Custom Logic for PP_ORDER_CONFIRMATION in your tenant, and
  • if the required validation/error handling is not supported there, treat this as a functionality gap and consider submitting it via Customer Influence.
Helpful references:
  • App Extensibility: Confirm Production Order (CO15) (Key User) [help.sap.com]
  • Confirm Production Operation [help.sap.com]
  • Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition [help.sap.com]
This response was prepared with AI‑powered assistance and validated against official SAP documentation current at the time of response. Please always verify against your system release.

 If this response answered your request, please mark it accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Re: RAP managed early internal numbering and static factory action - retrieve automatic guid
entry.content:
Hi @rammel_sapdev,

I used the first suggested solution suggested by both of you:

  1. Use a static factory action with the following steps
    1. create the instance(s) with some initial values (MODIFY ENTITIES CREATE)
    2. Update the content entity (MODIFY ENTITIES UPDATE) by iteration over the mapped from the first step with inline XML mapping  call.
      In this step I first used the %cid from the mapped list in the WITH-part of the update. But that did not go well and it is not needed as the guid key alone is sufficient.
  2. extracted the send functionality to another non-factory action

entry.title: Re: Standard Values Overview remains empty after assignin...
entry.content:
Thanks for the question, @KIRAN62.
 
Based on SAP Help for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the key point is that the Standard Value Key on the work center defines which standard value fields/keywords are available when the work center is assigned to an operation; SAP Help does not say that the work center itself should auto-fill the standard values overview just because the key was transported successfully. Standard value states that “standard values are planned values for the execution of an operation and are maintained in the operation,” and Basic Data states that after entering the standard value key in the work center, “standard values themselves are maintained in the operation in the routing.” [help.sap.com], [help.sap.com]
So, if the expectation is that assigning the Standard Value Key in the work center master should automatically populate the activity/parameter values directly in the work center, I could not find SAP Help documentation that confirms that behavior. The documentation instead describes this flow:
  1. Maintain the Standard Value Key in configuration and assign it to the work center. Standard value says the key is entered in the work center and is used to assign up to six data fields and keywords to the operation. [help.sap.com]
  2. Assign that work center to a routing operation. Create Work Center says that when you assign a work center to an operation, predefined data fields and keywords are added to the operation via the standard value key. [help.sap.com]
  3. Maintain the actual standard values in the routing operation. This is explicitly stated in Basic Data and supported by Manufacturing Master Data of Production Orders, which says the routing contains the standard values for execution of the operation. [help.sap.com], [help.sap.com]
A practical implication is that the transport can be fully correct even if the work center’s “Standard Values Overview” remains empty, because SAP Help describes the Standard Value Key mainly as the structure/assignment mechanism, while the actual values are maintained at operation level. [help.sap.com], [help.sap.com]
What I would suggest to you
  • Verify the behavior in a routing operation, not only in the work center app. Create Routing explains that the routing operation holds the technical details such as standard times, and Basic Data says standard values are maintained in the operation. [help.sap.com], [help.sap.com]
  • Check whether the required default values / default units of measure were maintained in the work center. Standard value says default units of measure for standard values can be entered in the work center Default values screen, and for in-house production operations the default unit of measure of work is maintained in Scheduling. [help.sap.com]
  • If you expected automatic population inside the work center UI itself, please share the exact app name and screenshot, because I could not find official documentation that this UI section is designed to auto-populate from the Standard Value Key alone. [help.sap.com], [help.sap.com]

Sources

  • Standard value — SAP Help Portal [help.sap.com]
  • Basic Data — SAP Help Portal [help.sap.com]
  • Create Work Center — SAP Help Portal [help.sap.com]
  • Create Routing — SAP Help Portal [help.sap.com]
  • Manufacturing Master Data of Production Orders — SAP Help Portal [help.sap.com]
  • Formula Parameter — SAP Help Portal [help.sap.com]
[AI transparency note]
This response was prepared with AI‑powered assistance and validated against official SAP documentation current at the time of response. Please always verify against your system release.
 
If this answer helped you, please mark the response accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: 2608 C4C Release Content
entry.content:
The 2608 pre-release information for SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud hasn’t been published yet. As soon as this information is available, you will be able to find it on "What’s New "notes and release highlights shortly before general availability. Please keep an eye on:

  • SAP Release Navigator 
  • SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps (Customer Experience)*

We will continue tracking the relevant 2608 release information in these portals. As soon as the materials and webinars are available, we will publish and share the details there.

*To access Value Map Groups, you must log in to SAP Community with an S-user ID or a Universal ID.

entry.title: Using Agentic AI as an Intelligent Decision Layer for SAP PM
entry.content:
A Conceptual Approach to Smarter Condition-Based Maintenance


Introduction
As manufacturing plants continue to adopt IoT and condition monitoring, equipment generates an ever-increasing amount of sensor data. Parameters such as vibration, temperature, pressure and flow provide valuable insights into equipment health.
The real challenge, however, is not collecting the data—it's deciding which sensor events actually require maintenance action.


Many organizations still rely on fixed thresholds or manual analysis by maintenance planners before creating SAP PM notifications. This article proposes a conceptual architecture where an AI Agent acts as an intelligent decision layer, ensuring that only meaningful events result in maintenance notifications.

Rather than replacing existing SAP PM processes, this architecture aims to improve the quality of maintenance notifications by introducing intelligence before notification creation.

1. From Equipment to SAP – The Current Approach

The journey begins with equipment continuously transmitting health information through multiple sensors. These sensor readings are transferred into SAP through an IoT platform or middleware.

While this provides excellent visibility into equipment health, every sensor event does not necessarily indicate a maintenance issue. Temporary operating conditions or minor fluctuations can generate alerts that require manual review, increasing the workload for maintenance planners.
The challenge is no longer collecting data—it's identifying which data actually deserves attention.


2. Proposed Architecture – Introducing an AI Decision Layer

The AI agent acts only as a decision layer. Once a notification is created, the standard SAP PM notification-to-work order process continues unchanged.

Instead of creating a notification for every sensor event, an AI Agent evaluates the incoming information before SAP PM is involved.
The AI analyzes equipment behaviour using historical trends, operational context and multiple sensor readings to determine whether the detected event represents:
      Normal operating behaviour
     Early equipment degradation
     A genuine maintenance issue


Only events classified as actionable result in an SAP PM maintenance notification.
Once the notification is created, the standard SAP PM process continues unchanged, including planning, work order creation, scheduling and execution.


Finally, technician feedback can be used to continuously improve future AI decisions, allowing the system to become more accurate over time.


The objective is not to replace SAP PM or maintenance planners, but to improve the quality of information entering the maintenance process.


3. Potential Benefits

Introducing an AI decision layer before notification creation could provide several operational benefits:

Reduced planner effort by filtering unnecessary sensor events before they become notifications.

Improved notification quality through richer context and better identification of genuine maintenance issues. 

Earlier detection of equipment degradation, enabling proactive rather than reactive maintenance.

Better equipment availability by reducing unnecessary downtime and focusing maintenance activities where they add the most value.

Continuous improvement, as technician feedback helps refine future AI decisions.

The exact benefits will naturally depend on each organization's equipment, processes and implementation approach.


Technical Considerations
Although this article focuses on the concept, several implementation questions remain open for discussion:

Should the AI run within SAP BTP or as an external service?

How should AI confidence influence notification priority?

What should happen when the AI cannot confidently classify an event?

How should technician feedback be incorporated into the learning process?

Conclusion

Condition monitoring has made equipment data more accessible than ever before. The next challenge is transforming that data into meaningful maintenance decisions.

Adding an AI decision layer before SAP PM notification creation offers one possible approach to reducing unnecessary notifications while improving maintenance efficiency. Rather than changing the existing SAP PM process, it enhances the quality of information entering it.

entry.title: Re: FAQ: Utilities Sales Process in SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
Thank you for the excellent session. Also very curios about how to fulfill utility requirements bundling commodity and non-commodity products and services. Here in North America, we mostly work with regulated utilities.
But utilities of all sizes, even down to smaller Co-Ops have the requirement to sell bundles of commodity&non-commodity products and services.
In this case, would we then recommend using the simple and complex sales process to cover commodity&non-commodity sales?
Or would we rather recommend the traditional Move-In/Out for commodity sales and complex sales for commodity&non-commodity?

Maybe this is already a question for the next Espresso session about the complex sales process! 🙂

entry.title: Re: Automatic sales order creation
entry.content:
Obrigado pela sua pergunta, @35400__ .
No SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, o processo padrão para contratos de serviços funciona de forma um pouco diferente do que criar automaticamente pedidos de venda de forma recorrente.

✅ Resposta curta

  • A criação automática de pedidos de venda a partir de um contrato de serviço não faz parte do padrão.
  • Em vez disso, os contratos de serviço usam planos de faturamento periódicos, que geram as faturas diretamente (sem necessidade de pedidos de venda intermediários).

✅ Abordagem recomendada

1) Utilizar contrato de serviço com plano de faturamento periódico

  • Configure os itens do contrato com um plano de faturamento periódico (por exemplo, mensal).
  • O sistema cria automaticamente linhas de faturamento para cada data.
  • Essas linhas representam as futuras faturas.
✔ Em resumo:
O sistema gera automaticamente as datas e valores de faturamento com base no plano.

2) Gerar automaticamente as faturas

  • Agende o job “Schedule Billing Document Request Creation”.
  • Esse job transforma as linhas do plano em solicitações de faturamento (BDRs).
  • A partir dessas, você gera as faturas (manualmente ou automaticamente).

3) Esclarecimento importante sobre pedidos de venda

  • Pedidos de venda:
    • São normalmente criados manualmente ou via integração
  • No contexto de contratos:
    • Eles são usados como ordens de liberação (call-off)
    • Não são gerados automaticamente de forma recorrente

✅ Quando usar pedidos de venda?

Use pedidos de venda somente se:
  • Houver necessidade de logística/entrega, ou
  • Você precisar liberar quantidades/valores contra o contrato
Para faturamento recorrente puro, eles não são necessários.

✅ Alternativa (cenário mais avançado)

Se o seu cenário for mais parecido com subscription (uso recorrente, consumo etc.), vale avaliar:
  • SAP Subscription Billing

✅ Resumo

  • ✔ Use contrato de serviço + plano de faturamento periódico
  • ✔ Agende o job de geração de faturamento
  • ❌ Não espere criação automática de pedidos de venda
  • ✔ As faturas são geradas diretamente a partir do contrato
Se puder compartilhar mais detalhes (tipo de contrato ou necessidade de logística), posso ajudar a refinar ainda mais a solução 🙂
Nota de transparência de IA:
Esta resposta foi preparada com o suporte de IA e validada com base na documentação do SAP Help Portal. Por favor, verifique sempre de acordo com a configuração e a versão do seu sistema.
 
Se esta resposta atendeu à sua necessidade, por favor marque-a como tal. Obrigado.

......

For transparency, translated to American English with a German accent, SAP's lingua franca ;-):
 

1) Question


Hello,
I would like to understand the process to automatically create a sales order from a service contract. I have tried, but I wasn’t successful.
What would be the best approach to automatically generate the sales order and the monthly invoice based on a service contract?
Scenario: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

✅ 2) Answer

Thanks for your question.
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the standard process for service contracts works a bit differently from automatically creating sales orders on a recurring basis.

✅ Short answer

  • Automatic creation of sales orders from a service contract is not the standard approach.
  • Instead, service contracts use periodic billing plans to directly generate invoices (via billing document requests)—without creating sales orders in between.

✅ Recommended approach

1) Use a Service Contract with a Periodic Billing Plan

  • Maintain your service contract items with a periodic billing plan (e.g., monthly billing).
  • The system automatically generates billing request lines for each billing date.
    👉 These billing request lines represent your future invoices.
  • This is the standard mechanism for recurring service billing.
  • Reference: Periodic Billing Plans for Service Contract Billing
✔ Key behavior:
  • “The system generates one billing request line for each billing date in the billing plan.” [help.sap.com]

2) Automatically Generate Billing Documents (Invoices)

  • Schedule the job “Schedule Billing Document Request Creation”.
  • This job converts billing plan lines into billing document requests (BDRs).
  • Then invoices can be created from these requests (manually or via automation).
  • Reference: Creating and Processing Billing Documents
✔ Key behavior:
  • Billing for service contracts relies on BDRs generated from billing plans [help.sap.com]

3) Important clarification about Sales Orders

  • Sales orders are typically:
    • Created manually, or
    • Created via APIs / integrations / specific scenarios
  • For contracts, sales orders are usually release orders (call-offs) and are not automatically generated by the system on a schedule.
  • Reference: Sales Contract Fulfillment
✔ Key behavior:
  • Sales contracts are fulfilled through sales orders created as releases [help.sap.com]

✅ When would you need Sales Orders?

You only need sales orders if:
  • There is a logistics or fulfillment process, or
  • You explicitly want to create “release orders” against a contract
For recurring service billing scenarios, they are not required.

✅ Optional alternative (if subscription-like behavior is needed)

If your scenario is closer to subscription billing (e.g., usage-based or complex recurring pricing):
  • You can evaluate SAP Subscription Billing integration
  • Reference: Subscription Management with Sales Billing

✅ Summary

  • ✔ Use service contracts + periodic billing plans
  • ✔ Schedule billing document request creation jobs
  • ❌ Do not expect automatic sales order creation as part of standard recurring billing
  • ✔ Invoices are generated directly from the contract billing plan

✅ Follow‑up (to refine guidance)

To confirm the best recommendation, could you clarify:
  • Are you working with service contracts or sales contracts, and
  • Do you require logistics execution or only recurring billing?

✅ AI transparency note

This response was prepared with AI‑assisted support and validated against SAP Help Portal documentation. Please verify against your system configuration and release.
 
If this response addresses your request to your satisfaction, please mark it accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Automatic sales order creation
entry.content: @AndreasMuno Thank you for the response! That makes a lot of sense. I was trying to fit this into the sales order model, but a periodic billing plan directly on the contract solves exactly what I needed. A couple of follow-up questions to close out my understanding: Is this flow supported for Brazil? The contract types available in Manage Sales Contracts are quantity and value contracts. I did some research and it looks like I'd need to activate a service contract type — how would I go about activating that? Can the periodic billing plan be set up directly on a service contract, or does it require a separate configuration step to enable it at that document type? Thanks again for the help!

entry.title: Re: Automatic sales order creation
entry.content: @AndreasMuno Thank you for the response! Is this flow supported for Brazil? The contract types available in Manage Sales Contracts are quantity and value contracts. I did some research and it looks like I'd need to activate a service contract type — how would I go about activating that? Can the periodic billing plan be set up directly on a service contract, or does it require a separate configuration step to enable it at that document type? Thanks again for the help!

entry.title: Re: CWE 377 Insecure Temporary File vulnerability in Crys...
entry.content:
Crystal Reports 11.5 is end of Life now, no more patches available.

And the CR SDK's are no longer shipped with Crystal Reports.

You can upgrade to CR for Eclipse now:

https://www.eclipse.org/downloads/

It will require you to update your project and references.

entry.title: Re: Crlf injection
entry.content:
See your other post:

CWE 377 Insecure Temporary File vulnerability in Crystal Reports Java Runtime 11.8.6.1371

MCP will come into picture when you start developing agentic use cases. Refer to the architecture center - https://architecture.learning.sap.com/docs/ref-arch/ae6821

entry.title: Public Cloud material master data migration, template missing Costing 1 - special procurement type
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I am doing material master data migration in Public Cloud. Because we want to use field: Costing 1 - special procurement type, but when I wanted to upload this field found that there is no this field in the standard material master uploading template, but only in MRP and Procurement tab. What's the logic behind, and how to proceed if we still want to use and upload field: Costing 1 - special procurement type. 

If we can NOT do that, will Uploading without this field + Mass change material work for this?

 

 

 

Thanks a lot!

Alice

entry.title: Re: Applying prerequisite SAP Notes separately to Product...
entry.content: Hi RaymondGiuseppi, thanks for your reply. It's really helpful.

entry.title: Recommended Approach to Change Fiscal Year Variant for an Existing Set of Books in SAP ByD
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to seek advice regarding the possibility of changing the fiscal year variant for an existing set of books in SAP Business ByDesign.

Currently, the set of books is configured with fiscal year variant 39 - Nov FY-1 to Oct, meaning that the fiscal year runs from November to October. We would like to change the fiscal year variant so that the fiscal year runs from May to April.

Journal entries and other accounting transactions have already been posted under the current fiscal year variant. We would therefore like to understand whether there is any SAP-recommended best-practice approach or alternative solution to change the fiscal year variant for the existing set of books.

We have also referred to KBA 2013755 - Fiscal Year Variant Cannot be Changed, which states: 

  • This change can be performed just for a new fiscal year, derived from a new set of books which would be using the desired Fiscal Year Variant.

Based on the above statement, we would appreciate any advice or experience regarding the following questions:

  1. Does this mean that we are required to create a new set of books with the desired fiscal year variant?
  2. If a new set of books is required, how should the financial data be transitioned from the existing set of books to the new set of books? Would this require a new migration of accounting data, including G/L balances, open AR/AP items, fixed assets, and other relevant balances?
  3. Other than creating a new set of books, is there any other SAP-recommended approach or workaround for changing the fiscal year variant when accounting transactions have already been posted?

We would greatly appreciate any guidance or experience from the community regarding the recommended approach for this scenario.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
HY

entry.title: Re: From SAP Integration Suite iFlow to MCP Tools: Exposing OData APIs to AI Agents
entry.content: @Ananta_Paul79 currently MCP server is available in enhanced and premium plans only.

entry.title: Re: Understanding Valuated and Non-Valuated Project Stock in SAP: What Really Happens During GR and

And I want to sync that Business Partner master data to S4 Hana Public Cloud.

Could you please guide me how can I do?

 

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: Re: Unable to release Network activity in CJ20N
entry.content:
Refer to SAP Note 678522 - FAQ 2: Data inconsistencies in the Project System

Look at Section 3 and 4 for possible fixes.
